question_text: A marble has a volume of 20 cm³ and a mass of 54 g. Explain whether the marble is more or less dense than water (which has a density of 1 g/cm³).
correct_answer: The marble has a density of 2.7 g/cm³ (54 ÷ 20), which is greater than water's density of 1 g/cm³, so the marble is more dense than water and will sink.
explanation: By calculating the marble's density and comparing it to water's density, we can predict whether it will float or sink. A density greater than 1 g/cm³ means the object will sink in water, which matches what we observe with marbles in real life.
